heading: Project Overview
text: Life skills sessions delivered on rapport building, collaboration, self-awareness, and assertiveness. Seven literacy and seven numeracy sessions conducted for Level 01 students. 224 study corners created to improve home-study environment. 2,225 regular and 8 irregular home visits conducted for progress tracking and absenteeism support. Staff development included a 3-day refresher training and a 1-day training for Community Coordinators.

heading: Quarterly Activity Summary
text: Multiple sessions completed across life skills, literacy, and numeracy. Study corners established at homes. Home visits strengthened student tracking. Staff underwent capacity-building training to improve facilitation and engagement.

heading: Key Highlight
text: 23 out-of-school adolescents admitted into formal schools after resolving documentation issues including Aadhaar, Samagra ID, and transfer certificates through coordination with families and authorities.

heading: Bal Panchayat Formation
text: Eight Bal Panchayat cabinets established. Students participated actively in elections and swearing-in ceremonies. The platform strengthened leadership, responsibility, and involvement in school development.

heading: Bal Panchayat Action - Soap Bank
text: Students identified lack of soap as a concern during the first Bal Panchayat meeting. They initiated a 'Soap Bank', contributed funds voluntarily, purchased handwash liquid, and made it available for all students.

heading: School Management Committees
text: Five School Management Committees formed in Pithampur, Housing Board, Achana, Sagor Kuti, and Bagdoon. First meetings completed. Orientation sessions planned for upcoming months.

heading: Donor Meeting and Field Visit
text: Bridgestone CSR team visited project sites, attended CLC sessions, reviewed children's work, and interacted with staff. Donors appreciated progress and expressed interest in using photos, videos, and stories for wider visibility.

heading: Hallmark Celebrations
text: International Literacy Day celebrated with 'Eddie the Eagle' screening across eight schools with 982 students. Hindi Diwas observed with posters, speeches, and essays; 64 students received prizes.

heading: Awareness Events
text: Substance abuse awareness programs conducted with MP Police in Bagdoon and Sulawad. Film screenings held, followed by sessions with officers on harmful effects. Students took a pledge to avoid substance use.

heading: Key Performance Indicators
text: 1,739 adolescents enrolled against a target of 1,400 (49% girls, 51% boys). Majority from Grades 6 and 7. 1,505 adolescents received at least 5 life skills sessions (~86% of targeted outreach). CLC sessions active across intervention areas.

heading: Budget Utilization
text: Planned budget for Q2 was INR 22,46,676. Utilization till Q2 was INR 21,83,808.

heading: Events and Workshops
text: 10 parent sessions, 33 teacher meetings, 8 hallmark day celebrations, and 2 awareness programs conducted this quarter, totaling 53 events.

heading: Timeline and Milestones
text: Refresher training completed for program staff and Community Coordinators. CLCs fully activated since June 25. Fun and Learning sessions initiated. Monitoring and reporting conducted regularly.

heading: Upcoming Priorities
text: Midterm assessments planned for CLC children. 34 foundational literacy and numeracy sessions and 13 life skills sessions scheduled. Continued community engagement through events, home visits, parent sessions, Bal Panchayat meetings, and consultations.

heading: Case Story
text: Kavita, a shy sixth-grade student, gradually gained confidence through storytelling and group activities. She participated publicly for the first time, showed improved communication, and demonstrated positive behavioral changes at home and school.

heading: Stakeholder Engagement
text: Parents engaged through meetings to understand the program's impact. Regular updates shared with BEO, BRC, ASHA workers, Anganwadi workers, and ward members to strengthen supportive environments.

heading: Learnings and Recommendations
text: Bal Panchayat formation, study corners, and community events worked well. Improvement needed in parent participation and home visit coverage. Recommendation to strengthen collaboration with government officials for visibility and support.
